definition: The number of moles of a substance dissolved in 1 liter (L) of solution.
examples: {'example': 'Calculate the molarity of a solution obtained by mixing 50 mL of 5 M Kâ‚‚Crâ‚‚Oâ‚‡ and 50 mL of 2 M Kâ‚‚Crâ‚‚Oâ‚‡ in acidic medium.', 'solution': 'v.f. of Kâ‚‚Crâ‚‚Oâ‚‡ = 6\nN_f = (Nâ‚پ Vâ‚پ + Nâ‚‚ Vâ‚‚) / (Vâ‚پ + Vâ‚‚) = ((5 أ— 6 أ— 50) + (2 أ— 6 أ— 50)) / (50 + 50) = 21 M'}

{'example': 'Calculate the normality of a solution containing 13.4 g of Sodium oxalate in 100 mL.', 'solution': 'N = (wt. in g/eq. wt.) / vol. of solution in litre\nHere, eq. wt. of Naâ‚‚Câ‚‚Oâ‚„ = 67 so N = (13.4/67) / (100/1000) = 2 N'}

definition: The number of equivalents of a substance dissolved in 1 liter (L) of solution.
examples: {'example': 'Calculate the normality of a solution obtained by mixing 50 mL of 5 M Kâ‚‚Crâ‚‚Oâ‚‡ and 50 mL of 2 M Kâ‚‚Crâ‚‚Oâ‚‡ in acidic medium.', 'solution': 'N_f = (Nâ‚پ Vâ‚پ + Nâ‚‚ Vâ‚‚) / (Vâ‚پ + Vâ‚‚) = ((5 أ— 6 أ— 50) + (2 أ— 6 أ— 50)) / (50 + 50) = 21 N'}

definition: A method of determining molar masses of solutes using osmotic pressure.
examples: {'example': 'Measurement of osmotic pressure provides another method of determining molar masses of solutes. This method is widely used to determine molar masses of proteins, polymers and other macromolecules.', 'solution': ''}
